From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

NFL team season

| * **[Eagles seasons](/wiki/List_of_Philadelphia_Eagles_seasons "List of Philadelphia Eagles seasons")** * [1934](/wiki/1934_Philadelphia_Eagles_season "1934 Philadelphia Eagles season") → | |

The **[1933](/wiki/1933_NFL_season "1933 NFL season") [Philadelphia Eagles](/wiki/Philadelphia_Eagles "Philadelphia Eagles") season** was the [franchise](/wiki/Philadelphia_Eagles "Philadelphia Eagles")'s inaugural [season](/wiki/List_of_Philadelphia_Eagles_seasons "List of Philadelphia Eagles seasons") in the [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League") (NFL). In a year with an unbalanced schedule, the Eagles saw the field a league-low total of 9 times in 1933, finishing the season with a 3–5–1 (.375) record.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Offseason

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1933_Philadelphia_Eagles_season&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Offseason")]

When Pennsylvania eased some of the [Blue laws](/wiki/Blue_law "Blue law") and allowed Sunday sporting events, Philadelphia and Pittsburgh became available for NFL franchises as they could play home games on Sundays. The Frankford Yellow Jackets[[2]](#cite_note-2) played their games on Saturday mostly when at home.

During the offseason, [Bert Bell](/wiki/Bert_Bell "Bert Bell") and [Lud Wray](/wiki/Lud_Wray "Lud Wray") were granted an expansion franchise in the NFL for the rights to Philadelphia. The previous team, the [Frankford Yellow Jackets](/wiki/Frankford_Yellow_Jackets "Frankford Yellow Jackets"), were inactive for two years so their rights were pulled by the NFL. They joined the [Pittsburgh Pirates](/wiki/Pittsburgh_Pirates_(NFL) "Pittsburgh Pirates (NFL)") and the [Cincinnati Reds](/wiki/Cincinnati_Reds_(NFL) "Cincinnati Reds (NFL)"), for a [$2,500 entrance fee](/wiki/Philadelphia_Eagles#NFL_in_Philadelphia_(1899–1931) "Philadelphia Eagles"). The Eagles got their name from the Blue Eagle, which was used by American companies to symbolize their compliance with the [National Industrial Recovery Act](/wiki/National_Industrial_Recovery_Act "National Industrial Recovery Act"), a program within [Franklin D. Roosevelt](/wiki/Franklin_D._Roosevelt "Franklin D. Roosevelt")'s [New Deal](/wiki/New_Deal "New Deal"). The NFL Eagles' original colors were a light blue and yellow.

The Eagles held their first training camp in [Atlantic City](/wiki/Atlantic_City "Atlantic City"), [New Jersey](/wiki/New_Jersey "New Jersey"). They scheduled their home games to be played at the [Baker Bowl](/wiki/Baker_Bowl "Baker Bowl") in Philadelphia, which was also the home of the [Philadelphia Phillies](/wiki/Philadelphia_Phillies "Philadelphia Phillies") of [Major League Baseball](/wiki/Major_League_Baseball "Major League Baseball"). The Eagles played at the Baker Bowl for three seasons before moving to the newer [Philadelphia Municipal Stadium](/wiki/Philadelphia_Municipal_Stadium "Philadelphia Municipal Stadium") in the south Philadelphia area.
